qualityChecks: cd plugins/slack && bun install && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint
description: Update the Slack plugin to use ToolError factory methods with structured metadata instead of raw ToolError constructor calls. This requires the latest SDK to be published first (done by a prior PRD). Update SDK dependency version.

userStories.title: Update Slack plugin SDK dependency to latest version
userStories.description: As a plugin developer maintaining the Slack plugin, I want to use the latest SDK version with all the new utilities and improved error factories.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/slack/package.json @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk dependency is updated to the latest published version
plugins/slack/package.json @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools dependency is updated to the latest published version
bun install succeeds with the new versions
bun run build succeeds
cd plugins/slack && bun run build passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes:
Edit plugins/slack/package.json. Update the version for:
- @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk: change from ^0.0.16 to the latest version (check what version was just published by the publish script — likely ^0.0.17 or whatever the new version is)
- @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools: same version

Then run `cd plugins/slack && bun install` to update the lockfile.

IMPORTANT: This story depends on the SDK being published to npm first. Check the current published version with `npm view @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk version`. If it hasn't been published yet, update to the workspace version by temporarily using a file: reference or just bump the caret range to include the current workspace version.

userStories.title: Migrate slackApi error handling to ToolError factory methods
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the Slack plugin to use ToolError factory methods for proper structured error metadata that AI agents can act on.
acceptanceCriteria: slackApi auth errors use ToolError.auth() with custom code 'not_authed'
slackApi 429 errors use ToolError.rateLimited() with retryAfterMs parsed from the Retry-After header
slackApi HTTP errors use ToolError.internal() or appropriate factory based on status code
slackApi JSON parse errors use ToolError.internal() with code 'invalid_response'
slackApi Slack API errors (ok !== true) use appropriate factory methods based on common Slack error codes
All ToolError instances have proper category, retryable, and retryAfterMs metadata
cd plugins/slack && bun run build passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes:
Edit plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts. The slackApi function (lines 362-428) currently uses raw `new ToolError(message, code)` without structured metadata. Replace with factory methods:

1. **No auth** (line 368): `throw new ToolError(...)` -> `throw ToolError.auth('Not authenticated. Open Slack in your browser and sign in.', 'not_authed')`

2. **429 rate limit** (line 402): `throw new ToolError(...)` -> Parse Retry-After header if available:
```typescript
const retryAfter = response.headers.get('Retry-After');
const retryMs = retryAfter ? parseInt(retryAfter, 10) * 1000 : 60000;
throw ToolError.rateLimited(`Slack API rate limited on ${method}`, retryMs, 'rate_limited');
```

3. **Non-ok HTTP** (line 407): Check status code:
- 401/403: `ToolError.auth(...)` 
- 404: `ToolError.notFound(...)`
- Other: `ToolError.internal(..., 'http_error')`

4. **JSON parse error** (line 414): `throw ToolError.internal('Invalid JSON response from Slack API', 'invalid_response')`

5. **Slack API error (ok !== true)** (line 424): Map common Slack error strings:
- `not_authed`, `invalid_auth`, `account_inactive`: `ToolError.auth(msg, errorCode)`
- `channel_not_found`, `user_not_found`: `ToolError.notFound(msg, errorCode)`
- `ratelimited`: `ToolError.rateLimited(msg)`
- `invalid_arguments`, `too_many_attachments`: `ToolError.validation(msg, errorCode)`
- Others: `ToolError.internal(msg, errorCode)`

Also check other files in plugins/slack/src/tools/ that may throw ToolError directly and update those too.

userStories.title: Migrate individual Slack tool error handling to factory methods
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want all Slack tool files to use ToolError factory methods consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: All ToolError usage in plugins/slack/src/tools/*.ts uses factory methods instead of raw constructors
Validation errors (missing params, invalid input) use ToolError.validation()
Not-found errors use ToolError.notFound()
Auth errors use ToolError.auth()
cd plugins/slack && bun run build passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes:
Search all files in plugins/slack/src/tools/ for `new ToolError(` and replace with the appropriate factory method.

Common patterns to look for:
- `new ToolError('...not found...', 'NOT_FOUND')` -> `ToolError.notFound('...', 'NOT_FOUND')`
- `new ToolError('...not authenticated...', 'not_authed')` -> `ToolError.auth('...', 'not_authed')`
- `new ToolError('...invalid...', 'VALIDATION_ERROR')` -> `ToolError.validation('...', 'VALIDATION_ERROR')`

Also check upload-file.ts which likely has validation errors for file type checks.

Note: Some tools may import ToolError directly. They should now import the factory methods too (they're static methods on ToolError, so the import doesn't change — just the usage).
